title: DaiMeng VTAM / Tactile-WAM: Predictive Contact Intelligence
description: DaiMeng internship research project: merge VTLA tactile-intuition survey and VTAM/Tactile-WAM proposal into one active Predictive Contact Intelligence track for dexterous manipulation.
summary: DaiMeng VTAM turns the VTLA tactile-intuition survey into an active internship project: From Reactive Manipulation to Proactive Manipulation. The merged track treats future tactile/contact latents as an Internal Contact Model and builds a Vision-Tactile-Action World Model with a Temporal Action-Token Shared-World architecture rather than simple vision/tactile feature concatenation. Fast contact/action rollout, visual/layer latent cache, contact-triggered sparse compute, and System-0/System-1 control are the execution path; validation is contact timing, slip/contact risk, actual/zero/wrong-action separation, latency/Hz/VRAM, and contact-rich task success.

subprojects.name: Universal Tactile Encoder
subprojects.summary: Learn a cross-device tactile latent from Marker Flow, tactile images, multi-finger timing, and contact events so VTAM can reason over touch without hard-coding one sensor.

subprojects.name: Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model
subprojects.summary: Use visual tokens, tactile/contact tokens, temporal action tokens, and shared-world tokens to predict future tactile latents, contact risk, and executable action chunks.

subprojects.name: Fast contact/action rollout
subprojects.summary: Keep heavy video generation as teacher supervision, visualization, or key-branch planning; run the deployment path through low-dimensional contact/action/tactile latents with cache, sparse compute, and distillation.

subprojects.name: System-0 / System-1 proactive contact control
subprojects.summary: Pair a 5-20 Hz predictive world model with a 50-100 Hz tactile servo that compares predicted tactile latent and current tactile latent to emit residual contact corrections.

subprojects.name: DaiMeng contact-rich benchmark
subprojects.summary: Evaluate stable grasp/slip suppression, in-hand adjustment, plug/pin insertion, card sliding, wiping, tool use, disturbance recovery, safety force, smoothness, and cross-object generalization.

subprojects.name: UMI2Render2Real workstation loop
subprojects.summary: Use the local workstation to turn UMI-device demonstrations into rendered robot data: scan object, scan scene, retarget UMI trajectories, render robot data, then measure the Render2Real gap and convert successful rollouts into semi-teleop data for VTAM/contact-intelligence training.

asset_alt: Three-system UMI World Model and VTAM architecture: System 2 UMI World Simulator, System 1 layered world action model, and System 0 DaiMeng VTLA contact servo.
asset_caption: Three-system VTAM architecture: DaiMeng VTAM owns the System-0 high-frequency tactile servo and its interface to System-1 layered world-action refinement and System-2 UMI world simulation.
details: Dashboard routing rule 2026-07-07: causal_robot_daimon checkpoint smoke, DaiMeng materialized robot189/Gamma roots, and Daimon queue/load gates are tracked here; UMI World Model should only consume their summarized evidence as Stage 1 input.

GE-Sim 2.0 reproduction path 2026-06-27: use the released GE-Sim-V2 repo and Genie-Envisioner-Sim v2.0 checkpoint as the first reproducible base for VTAM. Baseline goal is action-conditioned multi-view robot video rollout; VTAM extension adds contact/tactile latent prediction, pseudo tactile labels from contact/mask/proprio/object-slip signals, and a WAM state/action/contact rollout with actual/zero/wrong-action and contact-timing gates.

Origin: this was discussed with the Wuji team on 2026-06-11 as a Tactile-WAM proposal. The key shift is to stop treating tactile as only another observation channel; tactile should become the contact-grounding signal that turns video imagination into action-capable prediction.

Demo 3 first: use an external camera to observe human hand / arm motion, retarget that motion into the video model, and generate a robot arm + dexterous hand performing the same interaction. This should be a video-model demo, not necessarily immediate real-robot control.

Demo 1 second: from the generated robot video in the previous step, estimate tactile output: contact timing, contact patch, pressure / force proxy, slip risk, and contact-state transitions. This is the tactile world model layer.

Final target: extend the predictive world model into WAM. Instead of only predicting future video or tactile maps under given actions, the model should produce the action needed to realize the imagined contact-rich future.

Why this is not just VTLA: VTLA / Tactile Intuition remains the broad survey for tactile representation and benchmark choice. Tactile-WAM is a separate Survey card for the concrete proposal chain: generated robot video -> predicted tactile -> action-generating model.

Why this relates to Tri-View / Image-Layered IDM: the robot arm/hand video generator and layer decomposition provide the visual state backbone. Tactile-WAM adds a contact-state head and then an action head, especially for tasks where visual evidence alone cannot determine contact.

Why this relates to DexGello: DexGello can provide aligned real data for arm/hand/finger state, while Wuji tactile / glove streams can provide tactile supervision. Tactile-WAM can use that data to train video-to-touch and touch-to-action components.

Good first benchmark families: pressing, wiping, grasping, in-hand adjustment, cutting/peeling, and assembly/insertion. Choose tasks where tactile is genuinely necessary: occlusion, slip, force threshold, contact/no-contact ambiguity, or object deformation.

DaiMeng proposal update 2026-06-27: promote this card from Survey to ongoing Research because VTAM is now a live proposal/project track. The dashboard framing should use VTAM as the proposal name and Tactile-WAM as the technical mechanism: video-to-touch prediction plus action decoding for contact-rich manipulation.

Ongoing execution scope: build data alignment first (external/RGB-D video, wrist/hand video, Wuji qpos/tactile streams, robot state/action, timestamps, calibration snapshot), then run the three-stage demo: retargeted robot video generation, video-to-tactile/contact prediction, and action-head / WAM decoding.

DaiMeng proposal extraction 2026-06-27: the updated 2026-06-26 proposal is titled “面向灵巧操作的预测式接触智能研究 / From Reactive Manipulation to Proactive Manipulation / 基于 Vision-Tactile-Action World Model 的主动接触操作框架.” It reframes VTAM as predictive contact intelligence: future tactile latent is the Internal Contact Model, not an auxiliary sensor feature.

Proposal technical core 2026-06-27: high-efficiency VTAM should be a Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model, not generic vision/tactile feature concatenation or a plain video-model finetune. Token streams: visual tokens, tactile/contact tokens, temporal action tokens, and shared-world tokens. The model predicts future tactile/contact latent trajectories, contact risk, action latent/action chunks, and optional video rollout.

Proposal acceleration path 2026-06-27: use a fast contact/action path. Visual features or robot/object/background layer latents are cached at low frequency; tactile/contact latents and temporal action tokens roll out at higher frequency; heavy video/high-dimensional tactile decoders are used only for training supervision, visualization, or key planning branches. Add contact-triggered sparse compute and teacher-student distillation.

Proposal control architecture 2026-06-27: System-1 is the low-frequency contact world model, planned at roughly 5-20 Hz, predicting future tactile/contact state, risk, and action chunks. System-0 is a high-frequency tactile servo, roughly 50-100 Hz or higher, comparing observed tactile latent with predicted target tactile latent and outputting residual corrections such as finger micro-motion, normal-force adjustment, tangential slip compensation, or insertion alignment.

Proposal prior-work chain 2026-06-27: OmniViTac establishes large-scale vision-tactile-action data and tactile locality/contact-triggered value; OmniVTA shows future touch as predictive contact representation with TactileVAE and visuo-tactile world modeling; TacForeSight shows future touch as action guidance through force-conditioned tactile latent prediction and predictive tactile-conditioned policy. VTAM is the next step: an internal contact model plus active control loop.

Proposal evaluation update 2026-06-27: evaluation must go beyond task success and visual plausibility. Required metrics include tactile prediction error, contact timing error, slip/contact risk error, actual/zero/wrong-action gate, latency per step, control Hz, VRAM, teacher-student error, disturbance recovery time, slip count, safe force thresholds, action smoothness, and cross-object/position/tool generalization.

Merge decision (2026-06-27): Merge the old VTLA / Tactile Intuition survey card into DaiMeng VTAM/Tactile-WAM. VTLA remains the representation and benchmark substrate; the active Research card is the DaiMeng internship project focused on predictive contact intelligence.

Proposal core: The proposal goal is a Vision-Tactile-Action World Model that predicts future tactile/contact latents and action chunks before contact failure happens. The important design choice is a Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model, not just concatenating vision and touch features or finetuning a video generator.

Deployment contract: The fast path should roll out low-dimensional contact/action/tactile latents; heavyweight video generation stays as supervision, visualization, or selected planning branches. A pass requires latency/memory reporting, actual-vs-zero-vs-wrong-action gates, contact timing, slip/contact risk, and task-level manipulation success.

System-0 / System-1 loop: System-1 runs predictive VTAM at planning frequency, while System-0 runs high-frequency tactile servo control by comparing predicted tactile latent with current tactile latent and emitting residual corrections for slip, overload, misalignment, or impending contact failure.

World-model-guided VLA refinement update 2026-06-29: the BAAI seminar slide is effectively the policy-improvement loop version of VTAM. For contact-rich insertion, pressing, screwing, and assembly, let the pretrained VLA provide semantic/coarse motion, use a reward/contact model to detect the contact-rich phase, sample multiple candidate contact trajectories, score them through imagined VTAM/world-model rollouts, then stitch the selected segment back into the trajectory as post-training data. This should be tracked as an evaluation and refinement protocol, not just as a visual rollout demo.

UMI2Render2Real subproject 2026-06-30: from the personal presentation UMI2Render2Real pages, the intended loop is: large UMI-device datasets -> scan object -> scan scene -> retarget UMI trajectory -> render robot data with LeHome-style household/cooking scenes -> apply to the real robot while explicitly modeling the Render2Real gap. In DaiMeng VTAM this becomes the sixth subproject: a local-workstation pipeline for producing semi-teleop robot data and testing whether rendered trajectories plus tactile/contact supervision improve predictive contact intelligence.

details.text: [KNOWN][HIGH] OSMO is the direct prior for the shared tactile-carrier idea: the same stretchable magnetic tactile glove is worn by the human demonstrator and a biomimetic robot hand, reducing both visual and tactile embodiment gaps while transferring continuous normal and shear signals. Its wiping policy is trained only on 140 human demonstrations (about two hours, no real-robot training data) and reports 71.69% mean task completion versus 55.75% for vision plus proprioception over 12 rollouts. [INFERRED][HIGH] For VTAM, this validates matched sensor topology as a data-collection primitive but closes the novelty of "human and robot wear the same glove" by itself; the defensible extension is cross-device tactile latents, predictive contact/action modeling, and multi-task transfer beyond one matched human/robot glove and one wiping setup.

details: [KNOWN][HIGH] Event-camera cross-reference updated 2026-07-15: detailed evidence now lives in Survey: Event-Camera VLA / World Model Sensor Survey. [INFERRED][HIGH] DaiMeng VTAM should consume synchronized event features only after the Survey shows marginal value over 120 fps RGB, pseudo-events, and existing tactile streams; event hardware is not a VTAM mainline dependency yet.

[KNOWN][HIGH] SpikingTac cross-reference 2026-07-15: the Event-Camera Survey now tracks a separate neuromorphic visuotactile branch using a sealed marker-and-gel optical stack for 1000 Hz contact transients and 350 Hz marker tracking. [INFERRED][HIGH] DaiMeng VTAM may consume this as a candidate high-frequency contact stream only after independent reproduction against high-FPS RGB visuotactile and existing tactile/force baselines; this cross-reference does not reopen completed VTAM TODOs.

details.text: [KNOWN][HIGH] 2026-07-17 soft-sensor paper audit: E-slime is a published 2024 Advanced Functional Materials epidermal strain-sensing material, but the supplied image is a mixed research-trajectory slide rather than a clean paper figure. ML-sensor is now the published 2026 Advanced Science paper A Soft Mechanoluminescent Skin for High-Resolution Optical Tactile Sensing in Human-Machine Interaction. [KNOWN][HIGH] No retrievable DOI, preprint, or public project page was located for the supplied RoboTac Skin or Ther-Tac / T-Tac labels as of this audit; their high-information design slides remain useful as explicitly labeled presentation context, not published-paper evidence. [INFERRED][HIGH] For VTAM, the three retained slides span conformable contact skin, active thermal material sensing, and vision-native normal-force imaging, but none alone establishes predictive action or robot-policy gains.

references.title: GE-Sim 2.0 arXiv
references.notes: Paper reference for architecture, evaluation, and closed-loop rollout design.

references.title: Genie-Envisioner-Sim v2.0 checkpoint
references.notes: Released model checkpoint for reproduction and possible fine-tuning or tactile-head probing.

references.title: AgibotTech/GE-Sim-V2
references.notes: Open-source repo to reproduce before VTAM extension work.

references.title: GE-Sim 2.0 Project Page
references.notes: Reference baseline for action-conditioned robot video simulation and closed-loop evaluation before adding tactile/contact prediction.

references.title: User-shared XHS demo reference 1
references.notes: User-provided visual demo reference from the Wuji proposal discussion. Use as the inspiration for estimating tactile signals from generated robot video; content may require Xiaohongshu app/login for full viewing.

references.title: User-shared XHS demo reference 2
references.notes: User-provided supporting demo reference from the Wuji proposal discussion; keep as a pointer pending manual viewing in the app.

references.title: User-shared WeChat Channels demo reference
references.notes: User-provided demo reference for the external-camera human-hand retargeting idea: use human motion to control a video model of a robot arm plus dexterous hand.

references.title: Visuo-Tactile World Models
references.notes: Closest world-model reference: vision and tactile latents are predicted autoregressively under action conditioning, and the model is used for planning in imagination.

references.title: OmniVTA: Visuo-Tactile World Modeling for Contact-Rich Robotic Manipulation
references.notes: Reference for large-scale visuo-tactile-action data, tactile prediction, contact-aware fusion, and high-frequency tactile correction in contact-rich manipulation.

references.title: TouchAnything: Bimanual Tactile Estimation from Egocentric Video
references.notes: Reference for video-to-touch prediction: synchronized egocentric/wrist videos, bimanual hand pose, and dense pressure maps; supports the Tactile-WAM tactile-from-video head.

references.title: Dream-Tac: A Unified Tactile World Action Model for Contact-Rich Robot Manipulation
references.notes: Closest direct prior for VTAM: joint action, future visual observation, and tactile dynamics; use it as the first negative/positive baseline for the Temporal Action-Token Shared-World design.

references.title: Dream-Tac code
references.notes: Implementation reference for contact-gated visuotactile fusion, contact-aware attention bias, and cache-style diffusion acceleration.

references.title: TacForeSight: Force-Guided Tactile World Model for Contact-Rich Manipulation
references.notes: Useful for force-conditioned tactile latent prediction and predictive tactile-conditioned policy; maps directly to VTAM contact-risk and slip-risk heads.

references.title: VibeAct: Vibration to Actions for Contact-Rich Reactive Robot Dexterity
references.notes: Connects real vibrotactile sensing to contact/slip estimates and action learning; useful for System-0 tactile servo and contact-rich eval tasks.

references.title: FingerEye: Learning Dexterous Manipulation with Continuous Vision-Tactile Sensing
references.notes: Continuous vision-tactile sensing baseline; useful for deciding whether VTAM needs dense tactile tokens or only contact-event/tactile-latent tokens.

references.title: T-Rex: Tactile-Reactive Dexterous Manipulation
references.notes: Reactive tactile dexterity baseline; use for separating proactive VTAM prediction from purely reactive tactile control.

references.title: ART-Glove: Articulated Tactile Glove for Contact-Grounded Dexterous Interaction Capture
references.notes: Relevant for human-side tactile/dexterous interaction capture and for designing DaiMeng/Wuji dataset synchronization fields.

references.title: TacVerse: A Multi-Sensor Dataset and Benchmark for Cross-Sensor Vision-Based Tactile Perception
references.notes: Cross-sensor tactile benchmark; use to stress-test whether Universal Tactile Encoder generalizes across sensor geometry and marker/image modalities.

references.title: Cross-Sensor Touch Generation
references.notes: Directly useful for universal tactile latent design: translate between tactile sensor appearances instead of overfitting VTAM to one sensor.

references.title: Sparsh-X: Tactile Beyond Pixels: Multisensory Touch Representations for Robot Manipulation
references.notes: Representation baseline for multisensory tactile encoders; useful for Universal Tactile Encoder pretraining and ablations.

references.title: Im2Contact: Vision-Based Contact Localization Without Touch or Force Sensing
references.notes: Supports pseudo-labeling contact from video when tactile supervision is missing; useful for GE-Sim-to-VTAM tactile/contact head bootstrapping.

references.title: AnySkin: Plug-and-play Skin Sensing for Robotic Touch
references.notes: Sensor-side reference for practical tactile streams and cross-device deployment constraints.

references.title: DIFFTACTILE: A Physics-based Differentiable Tactile Simulator for Contact-rich Robotic Manipulation
references.notes: Simulation candidate for generating tactile/contact labels and for sanity-checking tactile prediction losses before real robot data is enough.

references.title: FACTR 2: Learning External Force Sensing for Commodity Robot Arms Improves Policy Learning
references.notes: Force-proxy reference for contact-rich manipulation when dense tactile is unavailable; useful as a baseline or auxiliary signal for VTAM contact-risk prediction.

references.title: World Action Models: The Next Frontier in Embodied AI
references.notes: Definition reference for WAMs: unify predictive state modeling with action generation, rather than learning only reactive observation-to-action mappings.

references.title: World Action Models are Zero-shot Policies
references.notes: Reference for joint video+action modeling as a policy route. Tactile-WAM extends this logic with tactile/contact prediction for dexterous manipulation.

references.title: 1X World Model: From Video to Action
references.notes: Industry reference for deriving robot actions from text-conditioned video generation; useful framing for video-model-to-action conversion.

references.title: DaiMeng VTAM proposal update, 2026-06-26
references.notes: Local DOCX extracted on 2026-06-27. Key additions: Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model, fast contact/action path, System-0/System-1 active contact control, and explicit latency/action-gate/contact-risk evaluation.

references.title: BAAI seminar note: world-model-guided VLA refinement
references.notes: User-provided seminar screenshot. Core loop: pretrained VLA handles semantic/coarse motion; reward model detects contact-rich phase; candidate trajectories are evaluated in imagined world-model rollouts; selected contact-rich trajectory is stitched into the VLA trajectory and used as post-training data.

references.title: Personal presentation: UMI2Render2Real pages
references.notes: Online presentation entry for the UMI2Render2Real pages. The excluded local source deck is `source-pptx/romoya_present.pptx`; the published section covers Project 2 UMI2Render2Real, object/scene scanning, UMI trajectory retargeting, robot-data rendering, Render2Real gap handling, and conversion of UMI data to semi-teleop data.

references.title: OSMO: Open-Source Tactile Glove for Human-to-Robot Skill Transfer
references.notes: [KNOWN][HIGH] Direct prior for the shared human/robot tactile-glove idea. OSMO uses 12 three-axis magnetic tactile sensors across fingertips and palm, open hardware/firmware/ROS2 interfaces, and a matched glove on the demonstrator and robot. The reported wiping result is 71.69% versus 55.75% for vision plus proprioception, not a twofold gain over the vision baseline; the experiment uses 140 human demonstrations, no robot training data, 12 rollouts, fixed camera/environment, and a Psyonic Ability Hand with Franka.

references.title: SpikingTac: A Miniaturized Neuromorphic Visuotactile Sensor for High-Precision Dynamic Tactile Imprint Tracking
references.notes: Candidate high-frequency contact stream for VTAM ablation; keep gated behind an independent SpikingTac reproduction and RGB/tactile baseline comparison.

references.title: Amoeba-Inspired Self-Healing Electronic Slime for Adaptable, Durable Epidermal Wearable Electronics
references.notes: [KNOWN][HIGH] Published in Advanced Functional Materials in 2024. Useful as a soft, self-healing strain-sensor material reference; it is not direct evidence for robotic contact prediction or control.

references.title: A Soft Mechanoluminescent Skin for High-Resolution Optical Tactile Sensing in Human-Machine Interaction
references.notes: [KNOWN][HIGH] Published in Advanced Science in 2026. The CMOS-read mechanoluminescent skin is a candidate normal-force/contact stream; published demonstrations emphasize handwriting and human-computer interaction rather than robot manipulation policy learning.

risks_decisions: Do not pitch this as only tactile prediction. The project needs the full chain: human-retargeted robot video generation, tactile estimation, and action output.
Synthetic tactile risk: tactile predicted from generated video can become visually plausible but physically wrong. Ground it with real Wuji / tactile sensor streams whenever possible.

Action-decoding risk: a WAM head must be evaluated on executable actions, not only video fidelity. The acceptance metric should include action plausibility, robot limits, contact success, and rollout consistency.
Data-alignment risk: tactile is high-frequency and local, while video is lower-frequency and global. The logger must preserve timestamps, calibration, contact frames, and sensor metadata.
Novelty boundary: VT-WM and OmniVTA already cover visuo-tactile world models. The differentiator should be external-camera retargeted robot-video generation plus tactile-grounded WAM action output.

Status decision 2026-06-27: move VTAM / Tactile-WAM from Survey to ongoing Research. It should now be tracked as an active DaiMeng/Wuji proposal project rather than only a tactile representation idea.

Proposal decision 2026-06-27: dashboard should track this as VTAM / Predictive Contact Intelligence, with Tactile-WAM as the video-to-touch-to-action path. The contribution claim is proactive contact manipulation through future tactile/contact latent prediction and fast action rollout, not only tactile sensing or multimodal fusion.

Architecture boundary 2026-06-27: avoid a video-first architecture where every control step decodes full future frames. Online control should use low-dimensional contact/action latents; video rollout is optional for training, visualization, and audit.

Acceptance boundary 2026-06-27: do not accept demos based only on pretty video or qualitative tactile maps. A passing VTAM demo must separate actual actions from zero/wrong actions, predict contact timing/slip risk, show latency/Hz/VRAM numbers, and improve at least one contact-rich manipulation task under perturbation.

Decision: retire separate VTLA Survey card: Keep one live card under Research. The merged card owns VTLA representation work, Wuji/Franka/HOI benchmark selection, VTAM architecture, and DaiMeng internship deliverables.

Risk: visual quality alone is the wrong success metric: The proposal should not pass because generated video looks plausible. It must pass contact prediction, action separation, latency, memory, servo stability, and contact-rich task success metrics.

[KNOWN][HIGH] OSMO novelty boundary 2026-07-17: a shared glove on the human and robot, direct transfer of normal/shear tactile signals, and policy training without robot demonstrations are already demonstrated. [INFERRED][HIGH] VTAM must not claim the matched glove alone; require cross-device or cross-morphology tactile normalization, predictive contact/action utility, and gains across multiple contact-rich tasks or unseen objects, with an ablation against unmatched sensor layouts.

Refinement decision 2026-06-29: BAAI-style imagined trajectory scoring belongs inside VTAM as a contact-rich policy refinement protocol. The reward/contact model must explicitly identify contact phase entry; otherwise the loop degenerates into generic video rollout ranking.

[KNOWN][HIGH] Image Context decision 2026-07-17: retain the RoboTac Skin, Ther-Tac and ML-sensor design/application slides with publication-status labels. Skip the mixed E-slime/RoboTac/T-Tac roadmap slide because it is a temporary composite, and skip the two MC-Tac slides because they describe a fifth, ambiguously identified work outside the four-item request. [INFERRED][HIGH] Revisit magnetic MC-Tac only after its exact paper/project identity is established.

timeline.target_note: Phase 0-3 path from offline VTAM/Tactile-WAM data preparation to temporal action-token model, fast rollout, and System-0 tactile servo validation.

milestones.label: Phase 0
milestones.note: Build offline VTAM/Tactile-WAM data path: external/robot videos, tactile/contact labels, action schema, synchronization, contact timing, and baseline predictors.

milestones.label: Phase 1
milestones.note: Train Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model and verify actual/zero/wrong-action separation plus future tactile/contact prediction error.

milestones.label: Phase 2
milestones.note: Distill to fast contact/action rollout; report latency, memory, Hz, and accuracy tradeoffs.

milestones.label: Phase 3
milestones.note: Close the System-0 tactile servo loop on Wuji/Franka-style manipulation and report task success, slip, recovery time, and force safety.
